LightningComponentBundle.description: Displays project tasks grouped by status. Project record pages scope to this project only (no project picker).

property.name: recordId
property.label: Record ID
property.description: On Account pages: Account Id. On Project pages: Project Id (tasks load for that project).

property.name: accountId
property.label: Account ID
property.description: Optional Account ID override (mainly for App/Home). On record pages, recordId drives context.

property.name: projectId
property.label: Project ID (override)
property.description: Optional. Normally inferred on Project record pages from recordId.

property.name: hideListViewFilterPanel
property.label: Hide List View Filters
property.description: When true, hides the list-style filter panel (project, account, PM, developer, status, priority).

property.name: portalExperienceMode
property.label: Portal / Experience detection
property.description: auto = detect Experience Cloud (recommended). experience (or portal/community) = always member-site UX. salesforce (or internal/lex) = never treat as Experience Cloud. Use experience on custom portal domains if the list behaves like internal Salesforce.

property.name: accountId
property.label: Account ID
property.description: Optional Account ID to filter tasks. Leave blank to show all tasks or use account filter message channel.

property.name: showAccountFilter
property.label: Show Account Filter (Deprecated)
property.description: Deprecated — ignored. Use the list filter panel Account field. This property cannot be removed from metadata while old page versions reference it.

property.name: useCurrentUserAccount
property.label: Use Current User Account
property.description: When true, defaults to the logged-in user's Account if no record/account is provided (useful for Experience Cloud)

property.name: context
property.label: Context (Deprecated)
property.description: Deprecated — prefer Portal / Experience detection or Is Salesforce Context on internal pages.

property.name: isSalesforceContext
property.label: Is Salesforce Context
property.description: Internal App/Home/Record pages: when true, LEX-style navigation. Experience Cloud: prefer Portal / Experience detection (salesforce or experience) instead — this flag is not shown there.

property.name: hideListViewFilterPanel
property.label: Hide List View Filters
property.description: When true, hides the list-style filter panel (project, account, PM, developer, status, priority).

property.name: portalExperienceMode
property.label: Portal / Experience detection
property.description: auto = detect Experience Cloud (recommended). experience (or portal/community) = always member-site UX. salesforce (or internal/lex) = never treat as Experience Cloud. Use experience on custom portal domains if the list behaves like internal Salesforce.

property.name: recordId
property.label: Record ID
property.description: Usually set automatically on record pages. Optional manual override on other pages.

property.name: accountId
property.label: Account ID
property.description: Optional Account ID to scope tasks (also use list filters or message channel).

property.name: projectId
property.label: Project ID (override)
property.description: Optional project Id when not inferred from the page.

property.name: showAccountFilter
property.label: Show Account Filter (Deprecated)
property.description: Deprecated — ignored. Use list filters. Retained for existing Experience Builder saves.

property.name: useCurrentUserAccount
property.label: Use Current User Account
property.description: When true, defaults to the logged-in user's Account when no other scope is provided.

property.name: context
property.label: Context (Deprecated)
property.description: Deprecated — use Portal / Experience detection (e.g. salesforce or experience).

property.name: isSalesforceContext
property.label: Is Salesforce Context (Deprecated on portals)
property.description: Deprecated — on portals prefer Portal / Experience detection (salesforce or experience). Leave false. Kept because existing Experience pages reference this property; runtime also honors portalExperienceMode.

property.name: hideListViewFilterPanel
property.label: Hide List View Filters
property.description: When true, hides the list filter panel (project, account, PM, developer, status, priority).

property.name: portalExperienceMode
property.label: Portal / Experience detection
property.description: auto = detect. experience = force member-site UX. salesforce = never treat as Experience Cloud.
